incidents.victim: Aqua Security (Trivy), Checkmarx, liteLLM, 1,000+ downstream organizations
incidents.vector: Exploited misconfigured GitHub Actions to steal a privileged access token, then injected credential-stealing malware into official Trivy and Checkmarx releases.
incidents.impact: Over 1,000 SaaS environments compromised with stolen SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens and cryptocurrency wallets. Mandiant expects widespread downstream breaches and extortion over coming months.

incidents.vector: Compromised endpoint management systems in Stryker's Microsoft environment, then deployed a data wiper across the corporate network.
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 devices, servers and mobile devices wiped across offices in 61 countries. More than 5,000 workers sent home in Ireland. CISA issued hardening guidance for endpoint management systems. FBI and DOJ seized Handala-linked websites.

incidents.vector: Drone activity in the vicinity of AWS's Bahrain data center region during the US-Iran military conflict.
incidents.impact: Amazon Web Services' Bahrain region was disrupted for the second time in a month, affecting cloud services for customers relying on the Middle East region.

incidents.victim: Organizations using Iranian timezone/Farsi locale systems
incidents.vector: Self-propagating worm (CanisterWorm) spread through exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, Redis servers and the React2Shell vulnerability, deploying a wiper payload against Iranian-locale systems.
incidents.impact: Wiper component destroys data on every node in detected Kubernetes clusters or wipes local machines if the system's timezone and locale correspond to Iran. Delivered via tamperproof blockchain-based smart contract infrastructure resistant to takedown.

incidents.vector: Compromised an administrative account days before deploying Pay2Key ransomware, then encrypted the environment and cleared event logs.
incidents.impact: Healthcare organization's systems encrypted. Researchers note the attack occurred concurrent with the onset of US-Iran military conflict and the group prioritizes destruction over financial gain. Pay2Key has collected $8 million from 170 victims since mid-2025.

incidents.victim: Current and former US government officials, military personnel, journalists
incidents.vector: Phishing messages disguised as automated support notices sent via Signal and other messaging platforms, tricking targets into linking attacker devices or providing verification codes.
incidents.impact: Thousands of Signal and messaging accounts compromised, giving Russian actors access to messages, contact lists and the ability to conduct further phishing. FBI and CISA issued joint warning.

incidents.vector: Compromised a private key used to authorize stablecoin minting, then illicitly created $80 million worth of uncollateralized USR tokens.
incidents.impact: Attacker traded illicitly minted tokens for approximately $24.5 million in ETH. USR stablecoin depegged from the US dollar to roughly $0.26. Platform paused operations.

incidents.victim: Operators of Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad botnets
incidents.vector: Compromised over 3 million IoT devices including routers and web cameras to build DDoS botnets used for extortion.
incidents.impact: US, Canada and Germany dismantled infrastructure behind four botnets responsible for over 315,000 DDoS attacks. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. Domains and servers seized.

incidents.victim: Organizations using Cisco firewalls and SD-WAN
incidents.vector: Exploited zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2026-20131) in Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center since January, along with multiple Cisco SD-WAN vulnerabilities, some exploited for over three years.
incidents.impact: Interlock ransomware group gained pre-authentication access to network management planes, enabling reconnaissance, custom RAT deployment and data encryption. Five of nine recently disclosed Cisco vulnerabilities confirmed actively exploited.

incidents.vector: Social engineering via messaging apps followed by delivery of malware disguised as legitimate programs (Pictory, KeePass, Telegram), using Telegram bots as command-and-control infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Malware enables screen and audio recording, file exfiltration and remote access on compromised devices. FBI issued flash alert tying the campaign directly to the group responsible for the Stryker attack.

incidents.vector: Hackers accessed Kaplan servers from October 30 to November 18, 2025 and exfiltrated files containing personal data.
incidents.impact: At least 230,941 people had Social Security numbers and driver's license numbers stolen. Breach notifications filed in seven states. Multiple class-action lawsuits initiated.

incidents.victim: Healthcare, government, hospitality and education organizations
incidents.vector: Fileless phishing campaign using copyright-infringement notices to deliver PureLog Stealer malware, primarily targeting organizations in Germany, Canada, the US and Australia.
incidents.impact: Data-stealing malware deployed using evasion techniques to bypass detection. Campaign demonstrates selective targeting of critical sectors with a structured delivery framework.

incidents.vector: Data analyst contractor stole corporate data including sensitive employee and compensation information using company-issued laptop access, then sent over 60 extortion emails after his contract ended.
incidents.impact: Company paid approximately $2.5 million in ransom. Contractor Cameron Curry found guilty of six counts of extortion, facing up to 12 years in prison.
